title: Environment, Shell State, Startup Files, and Sessions · Operations Recall
summary: Repair a command that works interactively but fails in automation by tracing environment and startup differences instead of adding global hacks. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for an environment-diff and startup-resolution notebook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish shell variables, exported environment entries, positional parameters, and child-process inheritance.
Trace aliases, functions, builtins, hashed commands, PATH search, and explicit paths with type and command evidence.
Identify login, interactive, non-interactive, and remote shell startup paths before editing configuration.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Shell Variables Environment · changed case
steps.instruction:
review case — check-report works in an interactive shell but fails from a service with PATH=/usr/bin:/bin, LANG=C.UTF-8, and HOME=/var/lib/lab. Trace command resolution and startup files without exporting a secret token. Competency probe: Distinguish shell variables, exported environment entries, positional parameters, and child-process inheritance.

Which action demonstrates Shell Variables Environment without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish shell variables, exported environment entries, positional parameters, and child-process inheritance.
